title: Fix media generation failures — robust error handling, retry logic, and Replicate API integration hardening

requirements.text: Audit and harden the backend Replicate API HTTP outcall for image generation: ensure the API key is read from a stable config store, the request payload matches Replicate's current REST schema (POST /v1/predictions with correct model version, input fields, and Content-Type: application/json), the IC HTTP outcall transform function is correctly stripping non-deterministic headers, and the response body is fully parsed before storing the result URL. Return a structured error variant (#ApiKeyMissing, #ApiError(text), #Timeout, #ParseError) instead of a generic trap so the frontend can display a meaningful message.

quotes: It failed with a 'generating' / 'generation error' state
Identify the top 3 likely root causes
Provide exact code/config changes to fix each cause

acceptanceCriteria: If the Replicate API key is not configured, the backend returns #ApiKeyMissing and the frontend shows 'API key not configured'.
If Replicate returns a non-2xx HTTP status, the backend captures the status code and body and returns #ApiError(text).
The IC HTTP outcall transform function strips Date, Set-Cookie, and other non-deterministic headers so consensus is not blocked.
A successful prediction response is parsed and the resulting media URL is stored in the user's gallery entry before returning to the frontend.
No unhandled traps occur during generation; all error paths return typed variants.

requirements.text: Audit and harden the backend Replicate API HTTP outcall for video generation using the same approach as REQ-14: correct payload schema, deterministic transform, typed error variants, and gallery storage before returning.

quotes: I attempted to generate media (image/video) from the website

acceptanceCriteria: Video generation returns #ApiKeyMissing, #ApiError(text), #Timeout, or #ParseError on failure instead of trapping.
On success the video URL is persisted to the gallery and returned to the caller.
The HTTP outcall transform strips non-deterministic headers.

requirements.text: Add a Replicate API key configuration endpoint to the backend admin interface. The admin must be able to set (and update) the Replicate API key via a dedicated admin-only backend function (setReplicateApiKey). The key must be stored in stable memory so it survives canister upgrades. A companion query (hasReplicateApiKey: Bool) must allow the frontend to check whether the key is configured.

quotes: Is the API key valid and not expired/revoked?

acceptanceCriteria: Only the designated admin principal can call setReplicateApiKey; all other callers receive #Unauthorized.
The stored key survives a canister upgrade (stored in stable var).
hasReplicateApiKey returns true after a key is set and false before any key is stored.
The key value is never returned in any query response — only its presence is exposed.

requirements.text: Update the Admin page to include a Replicate API Key configuration section. Display the current key status (configured / not configured) via hasReplicateApiKey, provide a password-type text input and a Save button that calls setReplicateApiKey, and show a success or error toast on completion. If the key is not configured, display a prominent warning banner on the Admin page.

quotes: Is the API key valid and not expired/revoked?

acceptanceCriteria: Admin page shows 'Replicate API Key: Configured ✓' or 'Not Configured ✗' based on hasReplicateApiKey.
A password input and Save button allow the admin to set or rotate the key.
On save success a success toast appears; on error an error toast appears.
If key is not configured, a warning banner is shown at the top of the Admin page.

requirements.text: Implement client-side exponential backoff retry logic in the GenerationTab component. After a generation error, display a 'Retry' button that re-submits the same prompt up to 3 times with delays of 2s, 4s, and 8s between attempts. Show the current attempt number (e.g., 'Retrying… attempt 2/3') during retries. Use a stable idempotency key (derived from prompt + timestamp) stored in component state so the same request is not double-processed.

quotes: Provide a safe retry strategy (exponential backoff, idempotency keys)
Client UX — Show progress + friendly retry button; store job-id and status endpoint

acceptanceCriteria: After a generation error a 'Retry' button appears alongside the error message.
Clicking Retry automatically re-submits the prompt up to 3 additional times.
The status indicator shows 'Retrying… attempt N/3' during retry attempts.
The idempotency key is fixed for the duration of the retry sequence and reset when the user types a new prompt.
After 3 failed retries the status shows a final error message and the Retry button is replaced with a 'Start over' option.

requirements.text: Improve the generation error UX on the GenerationTab component. When a typed error variant is returned from the backend (#ApiKeyMissing, #ApiError, #Timeout, #ParseError), display a specific, human-readable error message corresponding to the error type rather than a generic 'Generation error'. Map each variant to a distinct user-facing string (e.g., 'Service not configured — contact admin', 'Provider error — please retry', 'Request timed out — please retry', 'Unexpected response — please retry').

quotes: observed client message: what the user sees

acceptanceCriteria: #ApiKeyMissing displays: 'Service not configured — please contact the admin.'
#ApiError displays: 'Provider returned an error — please retry.'
#Timeout displays: 'Request timed out — please try again.'
#ParseError displays: 'Unexpected response from provider — please retry.'
No error case shows the raw internal error string to the user.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor file backend/main.mo.
The Replicate API key must never be exposed in any query response or frontend bundle.
Do not re-introduce any credit-gating logic on the frontend or backend.
nonGoals: Switching from the Replicate API to any other media generation provider.
Adding WebSocket or real-time polling for generation job status.
Modifying the Internet Identity authentication flow.
Adding new pages or routes beyond Admin page updates.
